pi_items.metadata_value: SRR10903401 (Real SARS-CoV-2 Illumina Amplicon subset) with injected low-quality reads
pi_items.source: bash execution of setup_task.sh confirms the real data curl URL returns a 404, resulting in a dataset containing only the 250 injected synthetic reads
task description aligns with standard bioinformatics role
task instructions and export_result.sh checks explicitly enforce Q>=20

privileged_info_summary: I audited the task and discovered a fatal defect. The setup script attempts to download the real SRR10903401 Illumina test dataset from an invalid GitHub nf-core URL, which returns a 404 Not Found error. Consequently, the real dataset is not downloaded, and the resulting FASTQ file only contains 250 synthetically injected low-quality reads (Quality 0). Because the dataset lacks any real reads, if an agent correctly applies the requested Q20 quality filter, all 250 reads are discarded. The verifier's logic strictly requires `0 < fq_reads` to pass the task, meaning a perfect execution will perpetually result in 0 points and failure, making this task completely unscorable. The data is entirely synthetic, contradicting the metadata claim of containing real amplicon data.
